item.title: Malaysia Is Running Out Of Caregivers And Time — Rashidi Yahaya
item.description:
Source: CodeBlue (Malaysia Health)
Malaysia requires a long-term care insurance framework that spreads risk across the population and creates a sustainable financing pool, built before the demographic wave hits full force, not after. The post Malaysia Is Running Out Of Caregivers And Time — Rashidi Yahaya appeared first on CodeBlue .
Matched: breaking(+3), urgent(+3), policy(+3), budget(+3), law(+3), parliament(+2), ministry(+2), war(+3), economy(+2), ai(+3), who(+2)

item.title: Outpatient Overload: Can Private GPs Relieve Malaysia’s Public Hospitals? — Dr Mohamed Rafick Khan
item.description:
Source: CodeBlue (Malaysia Health)
Scaling GP responsibilities is feasible, but only with coordinated training pathways, regulatory recalibration, and performance-based contracting to maintain clinical governance standards nationwide. The post Outpatient Overload: Can Private GPs Relieve Malaysia’s Public Hospitals? — Dr Mohamed Rafick Khan appeared first on CodeBlue .
Matched: policy(+3), ministry(+2), war(+3), ai(+3), who(+2), diabetes(+2)

item.title: Protect Children From Attacks On Schools — Malaysian Paediatric Association
item.description:
Source: CodeBlue (Malaysia Health)
The MPA urges the international community to move beyond words of sympathy and take decisive action to ensure that children everywhere are shielded from the horrors of war and violence. The post Protect Children From Attacks On Schools — Malaysian Paediatric Association appeared first on CodeBlue .
Matched: urgent(+3), law(+3), war(+3), ai(+3), who(+2)

item.title: LIAM Alleges Average Medical Insurance Claim Only RM12,000
item.description:
Source: CodeBlue (Malaysia Health)
In defending the RM100k annual limit under the Base MHIT plan, LIAM claims that the average medical claim is only RM12,000, even though many ITOs in the insurance industry regularly tout policies with RM1m or higher annual limits for medical emergencies. The post LIAM Alleges Average Medical Insurance Claim Only RM12,000 appeared first on CodeBlue .
Matched: policy(+3), war(+3), ai(+3), dengue(+3)

item.title: Health PSSC Moots Expansion Of Insurance Coverage For Mental Health Services
item.description:
Source: CodeBlue (Malaysia Health)
The Health PSSC recommends expanding insurance coverage for mental health services, warning that limited coverage is pushing patients to pay out of pocket and straining public hospitals. Preventive care remains largely excluded from insurance. The post Health PSSC Moots Expansion Of Insurance Coverage For Mental Health Services appeared first on CodeBlue .
Matched: parliament(+2), ministry(+2), war(+3), ai(+3), who(+2)

item.title: MOH Wants RM29m Additional Funding For Psoriasis Biologics
item.description:
Source: CodeBlue (Malaysia Health)
MOH spent RM17.35 million on psoriasis biologics in 2025, up 46.7 per cent from 2024, and plans to seek RM29 million more from MOF for six biologic medicines. Biologics are needed for a small group of patients with difficult-to-control disease. The post MOH Wants RM29m Additional Funding For Psoriasis Biologics appeared first on CodeBlue .
Matched: ministry(+2), ai(+3), funding(+2), who(+2)

item.title: Medical Officer Duties, Service Load Strain FMS Gazettement Training — Government Doctor
item.description:
Source: CodeBlue (Malaysia Health)
A government doctor says FMS gazettement trainees juggle medical officer duties, clinics, and administrative work during a six-month training period. This appears specific to Family Medicine, as other specialties do not routinely undertake MO duties during gazettement. The post Medical Officer Duties, Service Load Strain FMS Gazettement Training — Government Doctor appeared first on CodeBlue .
Matched: policy(+3), war(+3), ai(+3)

item.title: Expert: Gradually Replace PTDs In MOH With Public Health, Allied Health Professionals
item.description:
Source: CodeBlue (Malaysia Health)
A public health medicine specialist (PHMS) wants a study to identify MOH's admin posts held by PTDs that can be gradually filled by PHMS or allied health professionals trained in health system management. PTDs are still relevant in some areas in HQ/state. The post Expert: Gradually Replace PTDs In MOH With Public Health, Allied Health Professionals appeared first on CodeBlue .
Matched: policy(+3), ministry(+2), ai(+3), who(+2)

item.title: IHH Healthcare Malaysia Marks Third Consecutive Year in Newsweek’s World’s Best Hospitals 2026 Rankings
item.description:
Source: CodeBlue (Malaysia Health)
The continued recognition reflects governance systems that are firmly established across its hospitals, with patient safety and care standards applied consistently across specialties and services. The post IHH Healthcare Malaysia Marks Third Consecutive Year in Newsweek’s World’s Best Hospitals 2026 Rankings appeared first on CodeBlue .
Matched: court(+2), ai(+3)

item.title: SMC Ranked Malaysia’s No. 1 Hospital In Newsweek’s World’s Best Hospitals 2026
item.description:
Source: CodeBlue (Malaysia Health)
Sunway Medical Centre is home to more than 300 resident medical consultants and provides comprehensive care across over 80 medical specialties and 28 Centres of Excellence. The post SMC Ranked Malaysia’s No. 1 Hospital In Newsweek’s World’s Best Hospitals 2026 appeared first on CodeBlue .

item.title: Why More Seniors Are Choosing Assisted Living Over Staying Home
item.description:
Source: Medical News Bulletin (Health)
Medical News Bulletin - Daily Medical News, Health News, Clinical Trials And Clinical Research, Medical Technology, Fitness And Nutrition News–In One Place An increasing number of seniors are choosing assisted living communities over their private homes. While this shift may be shocking to some, many factors are behind the change. Those changing needs include a desire for comfort, safety, and social connection, which become ever more pronounced with age. Due to this, seniors and their families may […] The post Why More Seniors Are Choosing Assisted Living Over Staying Home appeared first on Medical News Bulletin .
Matched: ai(+3)

item.title: Do Senior Living Communities Increase Life Engagement?
item.description:
Source: Medical News Bulletin (Health)
Medical News Bulletin - Daily Medical News, Health News, Clinical Trials And Clinical Research, Medical Technology, Fitness And Nutrition News–In One Place Most people nearing retirement ask if having a home in a senior living community allows for even more engagement in everyday life. This question pertains to families and older adults. Being welcome and needed are and always will be crucial to every stage of life. Just as you likely want to be involved in local […] The post Do Senior Living Communities Increase Life Engagement? appeared first on Medical News Bulletin .

item.title: Eleven Reasons Early Intervention Through Child Counseling Is Beneficial
item.description:
Source: Medical News Bulletin (Health)
Medical News Bulletin - Daily Medical News, Health News, Clinical Trials And Clinical Research, Medical Technology, Fitness And Nutrition News–In One Place Children experience a wide range of emotions as they grow. Sometimes, these feelings become overwhelming or confusing. Early counseling support gives young individuals tools to understand themselves better. Addressing concerns early helps children thrive at home, in school, and with friends. There are many important benefits of seeking guidance early through child counseling. Encourages Emotional […] The post Eleven Reasons Early Intervention Through Child Counseling Is Beneficial appeared first on Medical News Bulletin .
Matched: ai(+3)

item.title: Top-Rated and Leading Hernia Center in Los Angeles for Essentially Surgical Care
item.description:
Source: Medical News Bulletin (Health)
Medical News Bulletin - Daily Medical News, Health News, Clinical Trials And Clinical Research, Medical Technology, Fitness And Nutrition News–In One Place It may be challenging to complete routine duties when one is experiencing distress as a result of a hernia. You may feel distress when you cough, lift, or walk. If you are seeking sophisticated and secure care, selecting a leading hernia center in Los Angeles may be a significant factor. A clinic that is highly […] The post Top-Rated and Leading Hernia Center in Los Angeles for Essentially Surgical Care appeared first on Medical News Bulletin .
Matched: ai(+3)

item.title: Securing Compensation for Long-Term Brain Injury Rehabilitation
item.description:
Source: Medical News Bulletin (Health)
Medical News Bulletin - Daily Medical News, Health News, Clinical Trials And Clinical Research, Medical Technology, Fitness And Nutrition News–In One Place A traumatic brain injury changes every aspect of a person’s existence in a single moment. While the initial hospital stay focuses on stabilizing the patient, the real challenge begins during the months and years of recovery that follow. Families can face mounting medical bills while also losing a primary source of household income. Obtaining enough […] The post Securing Compensation for Long-Term Brain Injury Rehabilitation appeared first on Medical News Bulletin .
Matched: ai(+3)

item.title: Hair Signals Linked to Thyroid Imbalance (It’s not Just Aging)
item.description:
Source: Medical News Bulletin (Health)
Medical News Bulletin - Daily Medical News, Health News, Clinical Trials And Clinical Research, Medical Technology, Fitness And Nutrition News–In One Place At some point, almost everyone has a moment where they get a little heart attack as you realize your hair fall has gotten really bad. You’re brushing your hair and think, Wait… was this always this dry? Or you tie it up and the ponytail feels thinner than it used to. Nothing dramatic happened. You […] The post Hair Signals Linked to Thyroid Imbalance (It’s not Just Aging) appeared first on Medical News Bulletin .

item.title: Long-Term Thinking in Aesthetic Surgery: Planning for the Next 10 Years, Not the Next 10 Weeks
item.description:
Source: Medical News Bulletin (Health)
Medical News Bulletin - Daily Medical News, Health News, Clinical Trials And Clinical Research, Medical Technology, Fitness And Nutrition News–In One Place The Problem With Short-Term Cosmetic Decisions Most people think about aesthetic surgery in weeks. When will the swelling go down?When can I return to work?How soon will I see results? Those are fair questions. But they are incomplete. Aesthetic surgery changes structure. Structure affects how a face ages. That means the real timeline is not […] The post Long-Term Thinking in Aesthetic Surgery: Planning for the Next 10 Years, Not the Next 10 Weeks appeared first on Medical News Bulletin .
Matched: ai(+3)

item.title: Best Electrolytes Without Artificial Flavors
item.description:
Source: Medical News Bulletin (Health)
Medical News Bulletin - Daily Medical News, Health News, Clinical Trials And Clinical Research, Medical Technology, Fitness And Nutrition News–In One Place Hydration Without Artificial Flavors and Dyes More buying decisions now start with ingredient-list inspection: roughly 75% of shoppers globally say they check labels. This shift is especially visible in beverages, where “clean label” and non-GMO expectations have moved from trend to baseline. Hydration used to be marketed with clinical, performance-heavy cues; today, better information and […] The post Best Electrolytes Without Artificial Flavors appeared first on Medical News Bulletin .
Matched: ai(+3)
